qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded file upload enables denial of service via memory exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unbounded avatar file uploads reaching io.ReadAll; it does not identify DeleteBranchPost, branch deletion routes, or missing protected/default branch checks.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Reverse proxy authentication header trusts arbitrary user identity without validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns trusted reverse-proxy authentication headers in internal/context/auth.go; it is unrelated to the protected branch deletion backend path in DeleteBranchPost.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CSRF protection bypass via state-changing GET requests enables unauthorized actions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns CSRF-prone GET routes for organization and milestone actions; the advisory is a direct POST authorization bypass for protected branch deletion with a missing server-side branch-protection check.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing authorization on attachment access allows any user to access any attachment
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unauthenticated /attachments/:uuid file access; it does not describe the branch deletion endpoint or the absent protected/default branch checks in internal/route/repo/branch.go.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: LDAP AdminFilter injection enables privilege escalation and authentication bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns LDAP AdminFilter injection in internal/auth/ldap/config.go and admin auth-source configuration; it is unrelated to protected branch deletion in DeleteBranchPost.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory root cause: DeleteBranchPost in internal/route/repo/branch.go allows a writer to delete protected or default branches because the backend lacks protected/default branch checks. The qualifying findings describe different vulnerable code paths and mechanisms.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2c6v-8r3v-gh6p targets internal/route/repo/branch.go DeleteBranchPost and route /branches/delete/*, where writer-authenticated direct POST requests bypass protected/default branch deletion safeguards.
Searches across the run artifacts found no qualifying report evidence for DeleteBranchPost, branch.go, /branches/delete/*, protected branch deletion, or default branch deletion.
The five qualifying findings are LDAP AdminFilter injection, reverse-proxy authentication header trust, unauthenticated attachment access, CSRF-prone GET state changes, and unbounded avatar uploads.
